title: Esperanza Education Fund Scholarship 2026: A Life-Changing Opportunity for Immigrant Students in the U.S.
summary: The Esperanza Education Fund Scholarship 2026 is now officially open, offering a transformative opportunity for immigrant students and children of immigrants to pursue higher education in the United States. Designed to empower academically driven students with financial need, this prestigious scholarship continues to make a meaningful impact by supporting students from Maryland, Virginia, and Washington, DC. As the cost of higher education continues to rise, scholarships like the Esperanza Education Fund play a crucial role in bridging the financial gap and creating access to quality education for underserved communities. If you are a student with immigrant roots and a strong desire to succeed academically, this scholarship could be the stepping stone you need to achieve your dreams. Overview of the Esperanza Education Fund Scholarship The Esperanza Education Fund was established with the mission of assisting immigrant students and their families in overcoming financial barriers to education. Through this initiative, selected students receive financial assistance that can be used toward tuition and other educational expenses. The scholarship is specifically targeted at students residing in: Maryland Virginia Washington, DC This localized focus ensures that the program directly benefits communities where immigrant families often face systemic challenges in accessing higher education. Application Timeline The application window for the 2026 Esperanza Education Fund Scholarship is clearly defined: Opening Date: May 1, 2026 Deadline: July 1, 2026 Applicants are strongly encouraged to begin their application process early to allow sufficient time for gathering required materials and preparing strong submissions. What the Scholarship Offers While specific award amounts may vary, the Esperanza Education Fund Scholarship provides financial support that significantly reduces the burden of college expenses. Beyond financial aid, the scholarship also offers recipients recognition and validation of their academic efforts and personal journeys. Recipients become part of a network of scholars who share similar backgrounds and aspirations, creating opportunities for mentorship, community engagement, and professional growth. Eligibility Criteria The scholarship is uniquely tailored for: Immigrant students or children of immigrants Residents of Maryland, Virginia, or Washington, DC Applicants are expected to demonstrate academic potential, financial need, and a commitment to their educational goals. While detailed eligibility requirements may include additional criteria, the core focus remains on supporting students from immigrant backgrounds who are determined to pursue higher education. Application Materials To apply for the Esperanza Education Fund Scholarship, candidates must prepare and submit specific application materials. These typically include: A completed application form Academic transcripts Personal essays Letters of recommendation The personal essay is particularly important, as it allows applicants to share their unique stories, challenges, and aspirations. This is an opportunity to demonstrate resilience, ambition, and the impact the scholarship would have on their educational journey. Selection Process The selection process for the Esperanza Education Fund Scholarship is highly competitive and holistic. Applicants are evaluated based on: Academic performance Financial need Personal character and leadership potential Quality of submitted essays and recommendations The review committee carefully assesses each application to identify students who not only excel academically but also show determination and a strong sense of purpose. Why You Should Apply The Esperanza Education Fund Scholarship is more than just financial support; it is an investment in your future. By receiving this scholarship, students gain access to opportunities that might otherwise be out of reach. This scholarship recognizes the unique challenges faced by immigrant families and aims to level the playing field by providing deserving students with the resources they need to succeed. If you meet the eligibility criteria, applying for this scholarship could significantly ease your financial burden and bring you one step closer to achieving your academic and career goals. How to Apply Interested applicants can begin their application by visiting the official application page.Make sure to review all requirements carefully and submit your application before the deadline. Deadline: July 1, 2026 APPLY HERE For more information about this opportunity, click here. For more global opportunities, click here. title: Global South Innovation Challenge 2026: Funding, Mentorship, and Global Recognition for Transformative Resilience Solutions
summary: The call for proposals for the Global South Innovation Challenge 2026 is now officially open, inviting innovators, entrepreneurs, and organizations to submit bold and transformative solutions that address some of the world’s most pressing challenges. This initiative is designed to support locally driven innovations that strengthen resilience, protect ecosystems, and create sustainable economic opportunities across communities in the Global South. Understanding the Context: Why This Innovation Challenge Matters We are living in an era defined by complex and interconnected global crises. Climate change, pandemics, financial instability, geopolitical conflicts, and food insecurity are no longer isolated issues. Instead, they interact and amplify one another, creating a fragile and unpredictable global environment. These compounded challenges disproportionately affect communities in the Global South, where vulnerability to shocks and stresses is already high. In this evolving risk landscape, traditional solutions are no longer sufficient. There is an urgent need for adaptive, locally grounded, and scalable innovations that can respond dynamically to changing conditions. The Global South Innovation Challenge responds directly to this need by empowering innovators who are closest to the problems and best positioned to design effective solutions. This initiative is supported by leading global partners, including Trafigura Foundation, Shockwave Foundation, and Munich Re Foundation, in collaboration with organizations such as the International Centre for Integrated Mountain Development (ICIMOD) and the SEACAR Alliance. Thematic Areas of the Innovation Challenge Applicants are invited to submit proposals under three major thematic challenges. Each theme targets a critical area of resilience-building and sustainable development: 1. Resilient Agriculture Innovations for Nature (RAIN) – Rural Challenge This challenge focuses on nature-positive agricultural innovations that enhance both ecological sustainability and community livelihoods. Solutions should address issues such as food security, sustainable farming practices, and environmental conservation. While the challenge is global, there is dedicated funding earmarked specifically for East African countries. 2. Resilience Innovation in Informality through Scaling Entrepreneurship (RISE) – Urban Challenge The RISE challenge seeks practical and scalable solutions tailored to informal urban environments. These may include innovations developed by grassroots organizations, urban startups, or social enterprises that improve living and working conditions in informal settlements. 3. Technology for Evolving Challenges in Humanitarian Contexts (TECH4Resilience) This theme focuses on leveraging advanced technologies, particularly Artificial Intelligence, to improve humanitarian preparedness and response. Solutions should enable faster, more targeted, and anticipatory interventions in crisis situations. There is special interest in TECH4Nature innovations, especially those originating from Southeast Asia. Who Can Apply? The challenge is open to a wide range of applicants, including: Non-profit organizations For-profit enterprises Local entrepreneurs and startups Established innovators and emerging changemakers Applicants are encouraged to submit solutions that are locally led and inclusive. A key requirement across all themes is that more than 50% of the beneficiaries must be women or youth, ensuring that the innovations promote equitable impact. What Winners Will Receive Successful applicants will benefit from a comprehensive support package designed to help scale their innovations. This includes: Tailored mentoring and expert guidance Monitoring, Evaluation, and Learning (MEL) support Strategic communication support Access to potential partners and investors In addition, winning projects will receive funding of up to US$50,000. This financial support will be awarded at the end of 2026 following a structured mentoring period and participation in a Virtual Leadership Academy. The funding will support a 12-month implementation phase from January to December 2027. Application Process Applicants must complete a consolidated application form via Submittable. The process is designed to be straightforward, but it is essential to carefully review the official guidelines before submitting. Proposals must be submitted in English, and only one application per organization is permitted, although organizations may collaborate on multiple submissions as partners. Key Timeline 1 May 2026: Call for proposals opens 13 May 2026 (15:00 CEST): Webinar for potential applicants End of June 2026: Applicants notified of results June – Mid-September 2026: Project development support period 6–9 July 2026: Virtual Leadership Academy November 2026: Winners announced at COP31 2026–2027: Ongoing mentoring and support Q1 2027: Induction workshop for winners Why You Should Apply This Innovation Challenge is more than just a funding opportunity. It is a platform for visibility, collaboration, and long-term impact. By participating, innovators gain access to global networks, expert mentorship, and the resources needed to turn ideas into scalable solutions that can transform communities. If you are working on an idea that addresses resilience, sustainability, or inclusive economic development, this is a unique opportunity to amplify your impact on a global stage. Application deadline: 22 May 2026 (23:59 GMT) APPLY HERE For more information on this opportunity, click here. For more global opportunities, click here. The post Global South Innovation Challenge 2026: Funding, Mentorship, and Global Recognition for Transformative Resilience Solutions appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Diamond Jubilee Research Scholarship 2026 for International PhD Students (Full Tuition Coverage)
summary: The Diamond Jubilee Research Scholarship 2026 presents an exceptional opportunity for high-achieving international students to pursue a fully funded PhD in the Faculty of Humanities Social Sciences. Designed to attract outstanding postgraduate researchers, this prestigious scholarship covers full tuition fees for up to three years of full-time doctoral study beginning on 1 October 2026 . Scholarship Overview This competitive award is aimed at individuals with strong academic backgrounds and innovative research potential. With only one scholarship available , applicants are encouraged to prepare thoroughly and submit a compelling application. Key Details Number of Scholarships: 1 Funding Coverage: Full tuition fees (international rate) Study Duration: Up to 3 years (full-time) Opening Date: 15 April 2026 Deadline: 13 May 2026 Start Date: 1 October 2026 Research Focus Areas Applicants must align their research proposal with at least one of the Faculty’s priority themes for 2026/27, including: Poverty reduction, inequality, and social exclusion Adolescent health and mental wellbeing Ageing, loneliness, and lifelong learning Ethical and legal implications of artificial intelligence Crime reduction and gender-based violence Globalisation, migration, and conflict Governance, democracy, and political systems Eligible Fields of Study The scholarship supports a wide range of disciplines within Humanities and Social Sciences, including: Public Health and Health Policy Psychology Social Policy and Social Work Education Law Politics and Policy Analysis Journalism and Media Languages and Translation Studies History and English Criminology Speech and Language Therapy Eligibility Criteria To be considered, applicants must: Demonstrate an excellent academic record (First-Class degree or equivalent, or a Master’s with Merit/Distinction) Show strong research potential through a well-developed proposal Align research interests with Faculty expertise Meet English language requirements (IELTS 6.5–7.0 or equivalent, if applicable) Candidates with slightly lower qualifications (e.g., Upper Second-Class degree) may still be considered if they present an outstanding research proposal. Important Financial Considerations While the scholarship covers full tuition fees, it does not include a stipend . Applicants must be prepared to cover: Living expenses (approximately £20,000 per year) Visa and immigration costs Travel expenses This scholarship cannot be combined with other university funding. Application Process Follow these steps to apply successfully: Identify your preferred research area Find a suitable supervisor whose expertise matches your topic Contact the supervisor with your CV and research proposal Obtain a Supervisor Statement Submit your application through the online system APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more scholarship opportunities, click here . Required Documents 1500-word research proposal 2-page academic CV Academic transcripts and certificates Two academic references Supervisor statement English language certificate (if required) Applicants who already hold an offer for 2026/27 must notify the Faculty via email before the deadline to be considered. Selection Process Applications will be evaluated and ranked by the Faculty. Successful candidates will be notified by early June 2026 . title: Transforming Care into Economic Power: ASEAN Grant for Caring Entrepreneurs (Up to $10,000 Funding)
summary: Across Southeast Asia, unpaid care work quietly sustains families, communities, and entire economies. From childcare to eldercare and household responsibilities, this essential work remains largely invisible and disproportionately carried by women. Now, a new regional grant opportunity is shining a spotlight on entrepreneurs who are turning this challenge into innovative, impactful solutions. Why This Grant Matters Unpaid care work is not the issue—it is vital to society. The real challenge lies in its lack of recognition, support, and fair distribution. Women across ASEAN spend significantly more time on unpaid care tasks than men, limiting their access to education, employment, and leadership opportunities. This is where local entrepreneurs step in. Small businesses, social enterprises, and community initiatives are already creating practical, culturally relevant solutions—from community childcare services to care-friendly workplaces. However, many of these efforts remain underfunded and underrepresented in policy discussions. About the Grant Opportunity The Caring Entrepreneurs ASEAN Grant Program , launched by Oxfam and ESCAP, aims to bridge this gap by supporting grassroots innovators working at the intersection of care and entrepreneurship. What You’ll Receive Up to $10,000 in funding Support for 5 months of documentation, storytelling, and reflection Opportunity to contribute to regional policy discussions Participation in a regional learning event in Bangkok (November 2026) What the Grant Supports This is a learning and documentation grant , not a business expansion fund. Selected grantees will focus on: Capturing and sharing their business journey Highlighting innovative care-focused models Engaging with policymakers and stakeholders Producing storytelling outputs (e.g., reports, documentaries, campaigns) What the Grant Does NOT Cover Infrastructure or large-scale expansion Equipment purchases Loans or business capital Long-term staffing unrelated to documentation Who Should Apply? This opportunity is ideal for: Small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) Social enterprises Cooperatives and community-based initiatives Especially those working on: Paid care services (childcare, eldercare, disability care) Care-responsive workplace practices Innovations that reduce unpaid care burdens Women-led and community-driven organizations are strongly encouraged to apply. Application Process To apply, you must: Submit a narrative proposal (max 4 pages) Include a budget plan (up to $10,000) Send your application to: info@oxfam.org.ph Use the subject line: “Application: Caring Entrepreneurs” 📅 Deadline: 10 May 2026 APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more grants opportunities, click here . Why You Shouldn’t Miss This This grant is more than funding—it’s a platform to amplify your impact, influence policy, and contribute to a more inclusive and equitable care economy across ASEAN. If your work is rooted in community, driven by purpose, and focused on care, this is your chance to be part of a regional movement for change. title: NYLC Youth Advisory Council 2026–2028: A National Leadership Opportunity for High School Changemakers
summary: The NYLC Youth Advisory Council (YAC) 2026–2028 cohort is now open for applications, offering high school students across the United States a powerful platform to lead, influence, and create real change. Designed as the premier youth leadership pathway of the National Youth Leadership Council (NYLC), this program empowers young leaders to become decision-makers, innovators, and national voices in service-learning and civic engagement. What is the Youth Advisory Council (YAC)? The Youth Advisory Council (YAC) is a select group of high school students who serve as experts, thought leaders, and co-creators within NYLC’s programs. With over 40 years of youth-centered impact, NYLC places young people at the heart of its mission—and the YAC represents the highest level of that commitment. Members don’t just participate—they actively shape programming, influence national conversations, and lead initiatives that impact communities across the country. Why You Should Apply Joining the YAC is more than a leadership role—it’s a transformative experience that equips students with real-world skills and national exposure. Key Benefits Develop authentic leadership skills through hands-on experience Become a subject matter expert in critical areas like climate, education, and public health Build a pathway toward national leadership roles, including mentorship and board-level opportunities Expand your network by connecting with passionate youth leaders nationwide Gain access to exclusive conferences, panels, and professional development opportunities Focus Areas for the 2026–2028 Cohort Selected members will specialize in one of the following impact areas: Community Health Education Climate/Environment Civics Democracy What You’ll Do as a YAC Member YAC members take on meaningful responsibilities across multiple initiatives: Youth as Solutions (YaS) Support youth teams through coaching, facilitation, and program development. Youth Leadership Report Conduct research and contribute to a national report highlighting youth perspectives and solutions. Youth Summit Plan and execute a national youth leadership event. National Service-Learning Conference Design sessions, recruit participants, and facilitate discussions at a major national conference. Content Creation Create impactful media content including podcasts, articles, and digital storytelling. Program Commitment Duration: 2 years (2026–2028) Monthly Commitment: 2–4 hours of virtual meetings and project work Events: Fall Leadership Retreat (2+ days) National Service-Learning Conference (4+ days) Travel Costs: Fully covered by NYLC Leadership Development Experience YAC members benefit from structured growth opportunities, including: Monthly research sharing to stay informed and engaged Hands-on facilitation and coaching practice Access to a curated library of learning resources Invitations to external conferences and thought-leader events Eligibility Criteria Applicants must: Be based in the United States Be in grades 9–12 Be at least 15 years old by December 31, 2026 Demonstrate passion for community impact and leadership Have interest or experience in NYLC’s Youth as Solutions (YaS) program Application Details NYLC will select 8 new members for the 2026–2028 cohort, with representation across all four focus areas. Interested applicants are encouraged to carefully review program expectations and submit their application as soon as possible. Deadline: Not explicitly stated (apply early to maximize your chances) APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more fellowship opportunities, click here . Final Thoughts The NYLC Youth Advisory Council is not just another extracurricular—it’s a launchpad for young leaders ready to make a national impact. If you’re passionate about driving change, collaborating with peers, and building a future rooted in leadership and service, this is your opportunity to step forward and lead. title: Health Equity Summit 2026 in Newark, New Jersey: Exploring the Future of AI and Technology in Healthcare
summary: The Health Equity Summit 2026 in Newark, New Jersey , hosted by the Congressional Black Caucus Foundation (CBCF), is set to be a transformative gathering for professionals, scholars, and advocates passionate about advancing equitable healthcare systems. This one-day summit brings together a diverse group of stakeholders to examine how emerging technologies, particularly artificial intelligence (AI), are shaping the future of healthcare delivery and access. As global conversations around healthcare disparities intensify, this summit provides a critical platform for dialogue, innovation, and collaboration aimed at achieving health equity across communities. Event Overview The Health Equity Summit is designed as an engaging, high-impact event focused on the intersection of healthcare, policy, and technology. Key Event Details Event Name: Health Equity Summit: New Jersey Date: May 16, 2026 Time: 10:00 AM – 3:00 PM Location: Donald M. Payne Sr. School of Technology, Newark, New Jersey, United States This in-person event will convene thought leaders and practitioners committed to addressing systemic healthcare challenges through innovative solutions. About the Congressional Black Caucus Foundation (CBCF) The Congressional Black Caucus Foundation is a nonprofit organization dedicated to advancing the global Black community by developing leaders, informing policy, and educating the public. Through its programs and events, CBCF works to address critical issues affecting underserved communities, including healthcare disparities, economic inequality, and education access. The Health Equity Summit is part of CBCF’s broader mission to foster meaningful conversations and actionable strategies that promote fairness and inclusion in public systems. Purpose of the Health Equity Summit The 2026 summit focuses on exploring how AI and emerging technologies can help bridge gaps in healthcare access and outcomes. It aims to: Highlight Healthcare Disparities Examine persistent inequities in healthcare systems Discuss social determinants of health Showcase Technological Innovations Explore how AI is transforming diagnostics, treatment, and patient care Identify opportunities for technology to improve healthcare delivery Encourage Policy Dialogue Engage policymakers in discussions about equitable healthcare reforms Promote inclusive policies that address underserved populations Foster Collaboration Connect professionals from diverse sectors Encourage partnerships between academia, industry, and communities Who Will Attend the Summit? The event is designed for a broad audience of stakeholders involved in healthcare and policy, including: Scholars and researchers Technology experts and innovators Healthcare professionals and administrators Policymakers and government representatives Community advocates and nonprofit leaders This multidisciplinary approach ensures rich discussions and diverse perspectives on health equity challenges and solutions. Key Themes and Discussion Areas Participants can expect in-depth discussions on several critical topics, including: 1. Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare The role of AI in improving diagnostics and treatment Ethical considerations in AI deployment Reducing bias in healthcare algorithms 2. Health Equity and Access Addressing disparities in underserved communities Expanding access to quality healthcare services The impact of socioeconomic factors on health outcomes 3. Policy and Advocacy Strategies for influencing healthcare policy Legislative approaches to promoting equity The role of advocacy in driving systemic change 4. Technology and Innovation Emerging tools and platforms in healthcare Digital health solutions for remote and underserved populations Integration of technology into existing healthcare systems Benefits of Attending the Health Equity Summit Attending this summit offers numerous advantages for professionals and stakeholders: Knowledge Expansion: Gain insights into cutting-edge healthcare technologies and policy trends Networking Opportunities: Connect with leaders across healthcare, technology, and policy sectors Professional Development: Enhance your understanding of health equity challenges and solutions Collaboration Potential: Build partnerships for future projects and initiatives Impactful Learning: Contribute to meaningful conversations that can influence real-world change Why This Summit Matters Healthcare inequities remain a significant challenge globally and locally. Events like the Health Equity Summit provide a platform to: Address systemic barriers in healthcare systems Promote inclusive and equitable healthcare practices Leverage technology for social good Empower communities through knowledge and collaboration By bringing together diverse voices, the summit fosters innovative thinking and actionable solutions that can drive long-term improvements in healthcare systems. How to Participate Interested participants are encouraged to stay connected with the Congressional Black Caucus Foundation for updates and registration details. Steps to Stay Informed Visit the CBCF official website Subscribe to their newsletter Monitor upcoming events and announcements Register early once participation details are released Final Thoughts The Health Equity Summit 2026 in Newark represents a critical opportunity for stakeholders across sectors to engage in meaningful dialogue about the future of healthcare. With a strong focus on AI, policy, and equity, the summit is poised to inspire innovative solutions that can transform healthcare systems for the better. Whether you are a healthcare professional, policymaker, technologist, or advocate, this event offers valuable insights and connections that can help shape a more equitable future. title: UNFPA Young and Emerging Evaluator Consultancy 2026 in Nairobi, Kenya (Hybrid): A Comprehensive Guide for Aspiring Evaluation Professionals
summary: If you are an early-career professional passionate about evaluation, research, and international development, the UNFPA Young and Emerging Evaluator Consultancy 2026 offers a powerful opportunity to build hands-on experience within a globally recognized organization. This consultancy role, hosted by the United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA), is designed to nurture the next generation of evaluators by involving them directly in high-level evaluation processes. This detailed guide walks you through everything you need to know about this opportunity—from responsibilities and qualifications to application steps and key deadlines. About UNFPA and the Independent Evaluation Office (IEO) The United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA) is the UN’s leading agency focused on sexual and reproductive health. Its mission is to ensure that every pregnancy is wanted, every childbirth is safe, and every young person’s potential is fulfilled. Within UNFPA, the Independent Evaluation Office (IEO) plays a crucial role. It operates independently from management and decision-making structures to ensure objective and impartial evaluations. The IEO: Conducts centralized evaluations at country, regional, and global levels Develops evaluation standards, methodologies, and frameworks Assesses the quality of evaluations across the organization Builds capacity and collaborates with global evaluation networks Promotes youth engagement and intergenerational learning in evaluation This consultancy directly contributes to these critical functions. Overview of the Young and Emerging Evaluator Role The Young and Emerging Evaluator (YEE) will support the implementation of centralized evaluations under the IEO’s multi-year evaluation plan. This role offers exposure to real-world evaluation processes in international development and humanitarian contexts. Key Details Location: Nairobi, Kenya (Hybrid) Contract Type: Consultancy Duration: 11 months (with possibility of extension) Deadline: 11 May 2026, 17:00 (America/New_York time) Key Responsibilities and Scope of Work The selected candidate will work closely with Evaluation Managers and contribute across all stages of evaluation. Responsibilities include: 1. Research and Documentation Creating and maintaining repositories of background documents Conducting preliminary document reviews 2. Data Analysis and Evaluation Support Performing preliminary financial analysis Supporting both qualitative and quantitative data collection and analysis Using relevant tools and software for data processing 3. Stakeholder Engagement Supporting stakeholder mapping and sampling Coordinating meetings, workshops, and advisory group engagements Liaising with internal and external stakeholders 4. Field and Operational Support Assisting in planning logistics for field missions Supporting evaluation teams during implementation phases 5. Reporting and Deliverables Contributing to drafting evaluation reports Supporting quality assurance aligned with UNFPA standards 6. Additional Duties Carrying out any other tasks assigned by Evaluation Managers Work Structure and Reporting The evaluator will report to Evaluation Managers within the IEO Monthly progress updates are required Deliverables must be reviewed and approved before final submission Payments are tied to successful completion of assigned tasks Required Qualifications and Skills Educational Requirements Applicants must hold a Bachelor’s degree in one of the following fields: Development Studies Public Health Social Sciences Business Administration Statistics or Data Analysis Demography or Population Studies Evaluation or related disciplines Professional Experience Minimum of 2 years of experience in evaluation-related work Experience with international organizations (especially UN) is an advantage Technical Skills Candidates should demonstrate: Strong analytical skills (statistical, financial, and survey analysis) Knowledge of monitoring and results-based management Familiarity with qualitative and quantitative research methods Excellent report writing abilities Communication Skills Strong written and verbal communication skills Ability to present complex findings clearly Language Requirements Fluency in English (required) Knowledge of Spanish or French (advantage) Ethical and AI Use Guidelines The consultancy includes strict guidelines regarding the use of Artificial Intelligence: AI tools cannot be used without prior written approval Any approved AI use must be fully disclosed Candidates must verify all AI-generated outputs Ethical standards such as fairness, transparency, and accountability must be upheld Additionally, candidates must not have prior involvement in programs being evaluated to ensure independence. Work Location and Legal Requirements The role is based in Nairobi, Kenya Non-Kenyan applicants must have a valid work permit Travel Expectations Travel may be required depending on evaluation needs. This will be determined by the IEO during the consultancy period. Application Process To apply, candidates must submit: Cover Letter Highlight relevant experience in evaluation, research, and data analysis Updated CV Submission Details Email : evaluation.office@unfpa.org Subject Line : “UNFPA IEO Young and Emerging Evaluator, Nairobi” Selection Process Initial screening Written test Interview Incomplete applications will be disqualified, and only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. Why You Should Apply This consultancy offers numerous benefits: Hands-on experience in global evaluation processes Exposure to UN systems and international development work Opportunity to work with experienced evaluation professionals Skill development in research, analysis, and stakeholder engagement Contribution to impactful global initiatives Final Thoughts The UNFPA Young and Emerging Evaluator Consultancy is more than just a job—it is a gateway into the world of international evaluation and development. If you are driven by data, passionate about impact, and eager to grow professionally in a global environment, this opportunity is worth pursuing. With a strong focus on youth inclusion and capacity building, this role is ideal for professionals looking to make meaningful contributions while advancing their careers. For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Job Opportunities on OFY here . title: PhD Scholarship in Education 2026 at Canterbury Christ Church University: Fully Funded Opportunity on Education Values and Impact
summary: The Canterbury Christ Church University (CCCU) is offering an exciting PhD Scholarship in Education: Education Values and Impact for 2026. This fully funded opportunity is designed for candidates passionate about exploring how education shapes values among children and young people and how these values influence society across generations. This scholarship is ideal for individuals interested in education research, youth engagement, social action, and intergenerational dialogue , with a strong emphasis on innovative and creative research methodologies. Scholarship Overview This PhD project focuses on understanding how education can create meaningful spaces where children and young people engage with: Civic values Political values Social values The research will also investigate how these values are perceived across different generations and whether commonly discussed societal divides are real or influenced by a “values perception gap.” The project builds on existing research, including insights from Dr Emily Lau , and aligns with studies highlighting how young people’s engagement in social action is shaped by compassion and societal expectations. Research Focus Areas The successful candidate will contribute to cutting-edge research exploring: The values perception gap between generations The role of education in shaping social and political attitudes How schools and community programs foster dialogue and understanding The impact of subjects like PSHE, Citizenship, and Religious Education The role of charities and community organizations in education The project also encourages creative and visual research methods , particularly when working with children and young people. What Does the Scholarship Offer? Successful applicants will receive a comprehensive funding package, including: 💷 Annual stipend: £21,805 🎓 Full tuition fee waiver for 3 years 📊 Annual research allowance: £500 👩‍🏫 Opportunity to gain teaching experience (150 hours across years 2 and 3) Beyond financial support, scholars will engage in impactful research that contributes to academic knowledge and real-world educational practices. Eligibility Criteria To apply for this PhD scholarship, candidates must: Hold an undergraduate degree and a Master’s (MA) in Education or a related field Demonstrate strong academic ability and commitment to research Have experience working with children and young people (essential) Show interest or background in community engagement, social or climate action, or education Meet English language requirements (e.g., IELTS if applicable) Be able to live in the UK within commuting distance of CCCU Be available to start full-time in September 2026 Applicants must also apply for both the PhD programme and the scholarship simultaneously . Application Process To apply, candidates should follow these steps: Contact a potential supervisor at Canterbury Christ Church University to discuss research ideas Download and complete the research proposal form Submit an online application through the university portal Upload required documents: Research proposal CV Academic references (2) Proof of qualifications IELTS results (if required) Shortlisted candidates may be invited for an interview with the academic panel. Application Deadline 📌 Deadline: 5 June 2026 Applicants are encouraged to prepare early, especially when developing a strong and well-aligned research proposal. Why Apply for This PhD? This scholarship offers more than academic funding—it provides an opportunity to: Contribute to important debates on education and societal values Work with experienced researchers and academic mentors Develop innovative research approaches with real-world impact Explore intergenerational dialogue and social change For those passionate about education, youth engagement, and social impact, this PhD is a powerful step toward a meaningful academic and professional career. For more information click here Discover more opportunities here The post PhD Scholarship in Education 2026 at Canterbury Christ Church University: Fully Funded Opportunity on Education Values and Impact appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Elephant Conservation & Research Funding 2027: Apply for Grants from the International Elephant Foundation
summary: The International Elephant Foundation (IEF) has officially opened applications for its Elephant Conservation and Research Funding Support Program 2027 , offering critical financial support to projects dedicated to protecting elephants, preserving their habitats, and supporting the communities that coexist with them. This funding opportunity is a major initiative for conservationists, researchers, NGOs, and field practitioners working to address the urgent challenges facing elephant populations worldwide. Overview of the Elephant Conservation Grant Program Each year, the International Elephant Foundation supports 20 to 25 projects globally , providing funding to initiatives that demonstrate clear impact, sustainability, and innovation in elephant conservation. The program focuses on practical, field-based solutions that are: Scientifically sound Ethically and culturally appropriate Feasible and scalable Inclusive of local stakeholders Applicants are expected to demonstrate that their proposed methods are either proven effective or strongly supported by research. Projects based on untested or ineffective strategies are unlikely to be funded. Funding Details Successful applicants can expect: 💵 Average grant award: $15,000 per year 🌍 Funding for both annual and multi-year projects 📊 Support for projects with measurable conservation impact The funding is designed to empower initiatives that can deliver tangible outcomes within a relatively short timeframe, while also building long-term sustainability. Priority Areas for Funding The program prioritizes projects that address key challenges in elephant conservation, including: Human-elephant conflict prevention and mitigation Habitat conservation and ecosystem management Community education and outreach programs Transboundary (cross-border) conservation efforts Anti-poaching initiatives and wildlife protection systems Reducing ivory and bushmeat trade Research on elephant health and diseases Projects that combine multiple focus areas and demonstrate systemic impact are particularly encouraged. What Makes a Strong Application? The International Elephant Foundation prioritizes proposals that: Are community-based with potential for growth Deliver immediate and measurable results Can be evaluated within 1–2 years Are led by experienced investigators with proven field capacity Show strong collaboration with local stakeholders Applicants are strongly advised to prepare thoroughly and align their proposals with these expectations to increase their chances of success. What the Grant Does NOT Cover To ensure funding is used effectively, certain expenses are not eligible, including: International travel to project sites Purchase of weapons or ammunition Paid informants Tuition fees for advanced degrees Overhead or indirect costs Applicants should carefully structure their budgets to comply with these guidelines. Application Process To apply, candidates must: Complete the online application form in English Prepare responses using the provided Word template (recommended) Upload all required supporting documents Submit the application before the deadline IEF also provides helpful resources, including: Grant-writing workshop recordings Presentation slides Sample application templates These tools are designed to guide applicants through the process and improve proposal quality. Required Supporting Documents Applicants must submit several key documents, including: Project location images (country and specific site) Photos of elephants in the project area Budget and timeline spreadsheets Organizational recognition documents (if applicable) CVs of investigators and co-investigators Permits, endorsements, and approvals Ethical clearance documents (e.g., IACUC, if applicable) Providing complete and well-prepared documentation is essential for a competitive application. Application Deadline 📌 Deadline: Friday, August 7, 2026 (11:59 PM PST) Late submissions will not be considered, so applicants are encouraged to start early and ensure all materials are complete before submission. Why This Grant Matters Elephants play a crucial role in maintaining biodiversity and ecosystem balance. However, they face increasing threats from habitat loss, poaching, and human-wildlife conflict. By supporting impactful conservation projects, the International Elephant Foundation is helping to: Protect endangered elephant populations Promote sustainable coexistence between humans and wildlife Strengthen conservation capacity worldwide For researchers and conservation practitioners, this grant represents a meaningful opportunity to contribute to global environmental protection while advancing their professional work. title: Energy Transition Fund Jobs 2026: Programme Officer Roles in Latin America & Africa (Remote, High-Impact Climate Careers)
summary: The Energy Transition Fund (ETF) is currently offering two high-impact career opportunities for professionals passionate about climate action and global energy transformation. These Programme Officer roles focus on accelerating a just and equitable transition away from fossil fuels in key regions: Latin America the Caribbean and Africa . These full-time, remote positions provide a unique opportunity to work at the intersection of climate policy, philanthropy, and global development , contributing to systemic change aligned with the Paris Agreement . About the Energy Transition Fund (ETF) Founded in 2019, the Energy Transition Fund is a collaborative funding initiative dedicated to supporting strategies that enable a rapid and fair transition away from oil and gas . ETF operates globally and works behind the scenes with philanthropic partners, civil society actors, and research institutions to drive meaningful climate action. Despite its low public profile, ETF is a growing and influential organization, offering professionals the chance to engage in impactful, large-scale climate strategies. Position 1: Latin America Caribbean Programme Officer 📍 Location: Mexico (Remote Work) 📄 Job Type: Permanent, Full-Time 🎯 Role Overview The Latin America Caribbean Programme Officer will lead strategic efforts to accelerate the energy transition in: Mexico (primary focus) Argentina (secondary focus) The role involves designing and implementing grantmaking strategies, managing partnerships, and identifying opportunities to strengthen climate action ecosystems across the region. Key Responsibilities Develop and manage a grantmaking portfolio in Mexico and Argentina Identify, evaluate, and support grantee organizations Build strong partnerships with stakeholders, funders, and civil society actors Lead cross-functional collaboration across ETF teams Monitor political, economic, and policy developments affecting energy transition Support fundraising, reporting, and strategic planning Required Expertise Strong understanding of energy policy, climate strategy, or economic transition Ability to build coalitions and drive systemic change Experience in philanthropy or grantmaking (preferred) Excellent communication skills in English and Spanish APPLY HERE Position 2: Africa Programme Officer 📍 Location: Nigeria (Remote Work) 📄 Job Type: Permanent, Full-Time 🎯 Role Overview The Africa Programme Officer will lead ETF’s work in: Nigeria (primary focus) Senegal (secondary focus) This role mirrors the Latin America position but focuses on African energy transition dynamics, supporting regional strategies and partnerships. Key Responsibilities Build and manage a grantmaking portfolio in Nigeria and Senegal Strengthen partnerships with civil society and philanthropic actors Provide expertise on regional energy and policy landscapes Identify funding opportunities and strategic risks Support collaboration across regional and global stakeholders Required Expertise Deep knowledge of African energy, policy, or civil society landscapes Experience in strategy, finance, or climate-related work Strong communication skills in English and French Ability to manage complex, multi-stakeholder projects Candidate Profile (Both Roles) ETF is seeking professionals who are: Holders of an undergraduate degree or equivalent experience Equipped with at least 5 years of relevant professional experience Strategic thinkers with strong analytical and communication skills Able to manage multiple projects and meet deadlines Comfortable working in remote, global, and multicultural teams Experience in philanthropy, grantmaking, or climate advocacy is highly valued. Compensation Benefits Both roles offer: 💵 Salary range: $81,553 – $91,677 USD (depending on experience) 🌍 Fully remote work environment 🧳 Opportunities for international travel 🖥️ Equipment and office support 🤝 Collaborative, inclusive global team culture Work Environment ETF operates as a globally distributed organization , with most staff working remotely. Employees benefit from: Flexible working hours Optional office reimbursements Exposure to international networks and high-level policy discussions The organization emphasizes professionalism, inclusivity, and compassion , creating a supportive environment for impactful work. Application Deadlines Latin America Caribbean Programme Officer: 18 May 2026 Africa Programme Officer: 20 May 2026 Why These Roles Matter These positions offer more than employment—they are an opportunity to: Influence global climate strategies Work with leading philanthropic and policy actors Contribute to a just and equitable energy transition Build a meaningful, purpose-driven international career For professionals committed to tackling climate change at a systemic level, ETF provides a rare and powerful platform.
